revision.comment: Bot: mark 17 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24167314344|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:稚児]][[zh:稚儿 (历史)]]
'''Chigo''' (稚児) has, broadly, the following meanings: * In its original sense, "chigo" refers to infants and toddlers (i.e., {{ill|Infant|ja|乳児|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Infant|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤ちゃん|1=Infant|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q998}} and {{ill|Toddler|en|Toddler|qid=Q1340307|lt=Toddler|1=Toddler}}). It is thought to be a contraction of the term “chinomigo.” Later, the meaning was expanded to include young children up to around 6 years old (prior to wearing {{ill|hakama|ja|袴着|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hakama|1=hakamagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30924452}} or having their hair tied). Boys and girls between the time they begin wearing hakama and untied hair up until their {{ill|genpuku|ja|元服|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genpuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Genpuku|qid=Q138321}} (coming-of-age ceremony) or when they start wearing {{ill|mongi|ja|裳着|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mongi|1=mogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626854}} were called {{nihongo|waraba|わらは・わらべ|qq=}}. * In large temples, "chigo" refers to young acolytes—see section [[#Chigo in Large Temples|Chigo in Large Temples]] below. *** By extension, the term came to denote young men who were objects of {{ill|male love|ja|男色|1=Nanshoku|nl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/male love|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanshoku|qq=}}. * In festivals, "chigo" refers to child participants—see section [[#Chigo in Festivals|Chigo in Festivals]] below. * Under the "Gōchū" system of the {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|qid=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, the term denotes the younger age group. In this system, children aged approximately 6–7 to 10 are classified as {{nihongo|kochigo|こちご|qq=}}<ref group="Notes">In some regions they were also called {{nihongo|warabe|わらべ|qq=}} chigo, Hego {{nihongo|yama|やま|qq=}} chigo, or Hego chigo.</ref>, and those aged 11 to 14 or 15 are classified as {{nihongo|osechigo|おせちご|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://blogs.mbc.co.jp/rekishi-no-chikara/583|title=郷中教育２８|date=2018-04-25|website=南日本放送|work=薩摩の教え|publisher=南日本放送|accessdate=2021-03-08|qq=}}</ref><ref group="Notes">This is the standard age division; it may vary by region.</ref>. == Chigo in Large Temples == [[File:Yoshitoshi Sojobo Instructs Yoshitsune in the Sword.jpg|thumb|200px|{{ill|Utenge|ja|烏天狗|1=karasu tengu|2=ja|3=烏天狗|4=de|5=Karasu Tengu|6=ko|7=가라스텐구|8=es|9=Karasu Tengu|10=sv|11=Karasutengu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utenge|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu Tengu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q863869}} and {{ill|Ushiwakamaru|ja|牛若丸|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushiwakamaru|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義経}} (Chigo at Kurama-dera)]] From around the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, in large temples of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} and the {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|qid=Q668188|lt=Tendai sect|1=Tiantai}}, a group of young trainee monks (approximately 12–18 years old) who did not shave began to appear, and these too came to be known as chigo. In such temple settings, children of royalty and high nobility were entrusted as etiquette apprentices (called "upper chigo"), those noted for their intelligence were employed as attendants (called "middle chigo"), and those with promising artistic talent (or who were sold by corrupt monks) were called "lower chigo." In the context of {{ill|Zen|en|Zen|qid=Q7953|lt=Zen Buddhism|1=Zen}}, this group was known as {{ill|Kasshoku|ja|喝食|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasshoku|1=Kasshoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419277}}. Their hairstyles were either {{ill|tairyō|ja|垂髪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tairyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the {{ill|chigo mage|ja|稚児髷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigo mage|1=chigo mage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596298}}, and they wore makeup similar to that of {{ill|Heian aristocratic|ja|平安貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian aristocratic|1=Heian aristocracy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482408}} women (sometimes including {{ill|Ohaguro|en|Ohaguro|qid=Q2016382|lt=Ohaguro|1=Ohaguro}}), and donned brightly colored garments known as {{ill|Suikan|en|Suikan|qid=Q11548540|lt=Suikan|1=Suikan}}. They sometimes also cross-dressed, making it difficult to distinguish them from girls. Since the large temples of the Shingon and Tendai sects, which served as training grounds, were located in mountainous areas and were restricted to men ({{ill|Nyonin Kinsei|en|Nyonin Kinsei|qid=Q11446290|lt=Nyonin Kinsei|1=Nyonin Kinsei}}), such chigo came to be seen as "feminine figures in a male society" and were frequently the objects of {{ill|Nanshoku|ja|男色|nl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanshoku|qq=}} (although the upper chigo were generally excluded). From the medieval period onward, even in Zen temple complexes ({{ill|Zenrin|ja|禅林|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenrin|1=Zenrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591187}}) and in the {{ill|Huayan|en|Huayan|qid=Q1198075|lt=Kegon sect|1=Huayan}}, chigo and those known as kasshoku were primarily objects of male love, shūdō, and shonen-ai. In particular, in the Tendai sect a ritual known as "Chigo Kanzō" was performed, during which the chigo were given names ending in "maru." Those who received such names were equated with {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|1=Avalokiteśvara}} and revered as sacred. For instance, in the {{ill|Huayan|en|Huayan|qid=Q1198075|lt=Kegon sect|1=Huayan}} at {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|qid=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}, there existed a monk who maintained relationships with approximately 100 chigo. == Chigo in Festivals == [[File:Chigo001.jpg|thumb|[[Fukagawa Shinmeigu]]'s chigo procession (in [[:en:Kōtō Ward|Kōtō Ward]], Tokyo)]] (In modern usage, "chigo" in festivals generally includes children (including pre-schoolers) who participate in festivals.) In contemporary festivals, young boys and girls (generally those below elementary school age) who wear distinctive makeup (often heavy makeup) and uniform or prescribed costumes are commonly referred to as chigo. However, whether they are called chigo depends on the festival organizers; for example, at the annual festival of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qid=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangu|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}}, the children in the "Yaotome" or "Dōji" category may not be called chigo, whereas in festivals like the {{ill|Himeji Yukata Matsuri|ja|姫路ゆかたまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji Yukata Matsuri|1=Himeji Yukata Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447453}}, even those appearing in plain yukata (without {{ill|Hakama|en|Hakama|qid=Q164668|lt=hakama|1=Hakama}}) may be referred to as chigo. == Costumes and Makeup == [[File:Chigo Costumes in Japan (1914-09 by Elstner Hilton).jpg|thumb|200px|Chigo from the Taishō period (circa 1914)]] The costumes of chigo are generally based on {{ill|Heian period attire|ja|平安装束|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period attire|1=Heian shōzoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482407}} (such as {{ill|Shinkansho|ja|神官装束|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkansho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平安装束|1=Heian shōzoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482407}} or {{ill|Miko clothing|en|Miko clothing|qid=Q17231335|lt=Miko attire|1=Miko clothing}}), or a greatly simplified version thereof; {{ill|Hakama|en|Hakama|qid=Q164668|lt=hakama|1=Hakama}} are considered indispensable. Boys often wear {{ill|eboshi|ja|烏帽子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eboshi|qq=}}, while girls typically wear {{ill|tengan|ja|天冠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tengan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Common accessories include fans (舞扇) and artificial flowers representing lotus, cherry blossoms, autumn leaves, etc. The makeup typically consists of a pattern on the forehead, known as {{ill|Ayatsuko|ja|アヤツコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒神|1=Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6455190}}, or a round mark called "Kuraihoshi" (both applied in black or red), with the nasal bridge painted white. Beyond this, the makeup may be minimal (only lipstick applied) or, alternatively, as elaborate as adult formal makeup, stage makeup similar to that used in {{ill|Shosagoto|en|Shosagoto|qid=Q11545264|lt=kabuki dance|1=Shosagoto}}, or even Western-style heavy makeup reminiscent of ballet. These costumes and makeup are not merely decorative; they are imbued with meanings of divinity, sacredness, and mystery, and serve as a rite of passage. == Classification by Type == In festivals, chigo can be broadly classified into three types: * Yorimashi Type * Dance/Performing Arts Type * Procession Type === Yorimashi Type === From ancient times, it was believed that divine spirits were more likely to descend upon very young children (under 6 years old). Thus, in shrine festivals, chigo who serve as "yorimashi" (physical vessels for the divine) appear. Today, they are treated as symbols of the festival; in most cases, they are boys, and usually only one, or at most three, are chosen. === Dance/Performing Arts Type === Young boys and girls who perform dances such as {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}}, traditional dance (舞楽), ennen, dengaku, and fuuryū, are often referred to as chigo. In the case of miko kagura, the miko wearing miko costumes may be called chigo, and those who perform as "norizu" on the taiko stage are sometimes also considered chigo. This type includes chigo who follow the tradition of the male trainee monks mentioned earlier. === Procession Type === This is the most commonly observed type. Chigo in this category appear during temple flower festivals (such as birth celebrations, kanbutsu-e, and Shaka’s birthday), on days commemorating the death of a deity (such as the {{ill|Okaishiki|ja|お会式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okaishiki|1=Oeshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4328808}}), and during occasional grand ceremonies such as the large-scale Buddhist festival ({{ill|Daibōkai|ja|大法会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法会|1=dharma assembly|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11555016}}) held once every few decades or centuries, as well as in processions at shrine festivals. In large-scale festivals, it is not uncommon for more than 200 children to appear. In addition, children's performance of {{ill|Tegomai|ja|手古舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tegomai|1=Tegomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496936}} may also be referred to as chigo. There is also an urban legend that if a child appears in a procession three times, they will be blessed with good fortune. == Festivals Featuring Chigo == [[File:Daidaikagura at Yahiko jinjya 010.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Echigo|en|Echigo|qid=Q4532967|lt=Echigo|1=Echigo}} Ichinomiya Yahiko Shrine's wife deity festival (featuring the dance "Kanju" performed in the dance hall as part of the Kagura)]] [[File:Gion Matsuri 2017-27.jpg|thumb|At [[Yasaka Shrine]]'s Gion Festival in Kyoto, a chigo accompanied by a nagatō-hoko and a bald head (Kyoto)]] [[File:Toyos001.jpg|thumb|A miko (girl priest) at [[Suwa Shrine]] (Toyoka City)]] [[File:Tekom001.jpg|thumb|Chigo performing Tegomai at the Ise Town Gion Festival (Nakanjo Town)]] *Note: For details on festivals such as {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}}, {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|qid=Q844110|lt=Hinamatsuri|1=Hinamatsuri}}, {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|qid=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e|1=Buddha's Birthday}}, {{ill|Okaishiki|ja|お会式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okaishiki|1=Oeshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4328808}}, and {{ill|Tegomai|ja|手古舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tegomai|1=Tegomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496936}}, please refer to their respective entries. ==== New Calendar (Gregorian) ==== * 1 January ** January 4: {{ill|Toyohashi Hana Matsuri|ja|豊橋花祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohashi Hana Matsuri|1=Toyohashi Hana Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633857}} (Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture) ** January 15: {{ill|Chakkirako|en|Chakkirako|qid=Q11318123|lt=Chakkirako|1=Chakkirako}} (Miura City, Kanagawa Prefecture) ** January 27: {{ill|Tayutama Hashira-sai|ja|太玉柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tayutama Hashira-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nakaku, Nagoya City, Aichi Prefecture, at {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛知県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛知縣護國神社}}) ** January 28: {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q985655}} (Hino City, Tokyo, at {{ill|Kōfuku-ji (Hino)|ja|高幡不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfuku-ji (Hino)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金剛寺 (日野市)|1=Takahata Fu-dō Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6587312}}) * February ** February 10–11: {{ill|Onisai|ja|鬼祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onisai|1=Onisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11673522}} (Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture, at {{ill|Ankumi Kondo Shrine|ja|安久美神戸神明社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankumi Kondo Shrine|1=Akumi Kanbe Shinmeisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450181}}) ** February 11: {{ill|Ohinasama Shisai|ja|おひな様始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohinasama Shisai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yanagawa City, Fukuoka Prefecture) ** February 17–20: {{ill|Enburi|ja|えんぶり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} (Hachinohe City, Aomori Prefecture) ** Late February: {{ill|Katsūra Big Hinasai|ja|かつうらビッグひな祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsūra Big Hinasai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ビッグひな祭り|1=Katsūra Big Hina-matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11330706}} (Katsura City, Chiba Prefecture) * March ** March 3: {{ill|Urasaki Bishamondo Hadaraoshi Dai-sai|ja|浦佐毘沙門堂裸押合大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urasaki Bishamondo Hadaraoshi Dai-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Pukōji|ja|普光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pukōji|1=Fukōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11089587}} in Minami-Urasaki, 【check location】) ** Early March: {{ill|Ishidan Hinamatsuri|ja|石段ひなまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishidan Hinamatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shibukawa City, Ikaho Onsen) ** Early March: Vernal Regular Festival (at {{ill|Sakuramori Inari Shrine|ja|笹森稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramori Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kanraku Town) ** Early March: {{ill|Maitou-sai|ja|祭頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maitou-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿島神宮|1=Kashima Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q706499}} (at {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|qid=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} in Kashima City) ** Early March: {{ill|Big Hinasai|ja|ビッグひな祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Big Hinasai|1=Katsūra Big Hina-matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11330706}} (in Naruhon, Katsura Town) ** March 15: {{ill|Nehan Matsuri|ja|ねはんまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehan Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in Kitamura, Namie Town) ** Mid-March: {{ill|Enburi Water Parade|ja|おひな様水上パレード|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi Water Parade|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yanagawa City, Fukuoka Prefecture) ** March 25: {{ill|Kawachi no Haru Goto|ja|河内の春ごと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Haru Goto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at the {{ill|Natanomiyaki Festival|ja|菜種御供大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natanomiyaki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) (at {{ill|Dōmyōji Tennmangu|ja|道明寺天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Tennmangu|1=Dōmyōji Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641744}}) ** Late March: {{ill|Hatsu-uma Dai-sai|ja|初午大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu-uma Dai-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Katsuragi Shrine|ja|冠稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shrine|1=Kanmuri Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395055}} in Otai City) ** Last Sunday in March: {{ill|Chie Bunshu Matsuri|ja|智恵文殊まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chie Bunshu Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Zaibunji|ja|財賀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaibunji|1=Zaika-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634644}} in Toyokawa City, Aichi Prefecture) * April ** April 1: Vernal Regular Festival (at {{ill|Sakaminomori Shrine|ja|咲前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaminomori Shrine|1=Sakisaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q130456565}} in Annaka City, Gunma Prefecture) ** April 1: Kuwana Seiten Dai-sai (at {{ill|Daihuku-ji|ja|大福田寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihuku-ji|1=Daifukuden-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438353}} in Kuwana City, Aichi Prefecture) ** April 1: Chanchan Matsuri (at {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|qid=Q245731|lt=Yamato Shrine|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture) ** April 3 and 5: {{ill|Chigo Kenkō Kigan-sai|ja|稚児健康祈願祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Kenkō Kigan-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|en|Mishima Taisha|qid=Q650772|lt=Mitsushima Shrine|1=Mishima Taisha}} in Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture) ** April 4: {{ill|Kagura Matsuri|ja|神楽祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagura Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Sabishi Shrine|ja|佐備神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabishi Shrine|1=Sabi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11383015}} in Tatebayashi City, Wakayama Prefecture) ** April 5: {{ill|Shizuka Asama Shrine Niju-sai Matsuri|ja|静岡浅間神社廿日会祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuka Asama Shrine Niju-sai Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} / Classical Chigo Procession (at Shizuoka City) ** Early April: {{ill|Tsurumai Hana Matsuri|ja|鶴舞花まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumai Hana Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at Ageo City, Chiba Prefecture) ** Early April: Vernal Regular Festival (at {{ill|Suwano Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwano Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2370082}} in Tomioka City, Gunma Prefecture) ** Early April: {{ill|Gogyō no Mai|ja|五行の舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogyō no Mai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Chikatsu Shrines|ja|血方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Oyama City) ** Early April: {{ill|Aoi Matsuri|en|Aoi Matsuri|qid=Q773085|lt=Aoi Matsuri|1=Aoi Matsuri}} (at {{ill|Serada Tōshō-gū|en|Serada Tōshō-gū|qid=Q11362533|lt=Yamato Shrine|1=Serada Tōshō-gū}} in Otai City, Gunma Prefecture) ** April 10: {{ill|Tenshin Shrine Kagura|ja|天津神社舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Shrine Kagura|1=Tenshin Shrine Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442800}} (at {{ill|Tenshin Shrine|ja|天津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Shrine|1=Amatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16276796}} in Itoigawa City, Niigata Prefecture) – Designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (officially titled "Itōigawa / Nōsei no Kagura") ** April 13–15: Vernal Regular Festival (at {{ill|Seneimatsuri|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seneimatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385469}} at Kumano Hongū Taisha in Tanabe City, Wakayama Prefecture) ** April 17: {{ill|Marumage Matsuri|ja|まるまげ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marumage Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at Toyama City, Toyama Prefecture) ** April 18: {{ill|Saito Ōkami Rei-sai|ja|妻戸大神例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saito Ōkami Rei-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} in Nishikubaru, Niigata Prefecture) – Designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (officially titled "Yahiko Shrine Tōrō Oshito Kagura") ** April 19: {{ill|Chigo Hōhei Shinkaji|ja|稚児奉幣神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Hōhei Shinkaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (at {{ill|Hokai Shrine|ja|日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture) ** April 19: Vernal Regular Festival (at {{ill|Kuragano Shrine|ja|倉賀野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuragano Shrine|1=Kuragano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131275534}} in Takasaki City, Gunma Prefecture) ** Mid-April: {{ill|Oka {{ill|Ashita no Chigo Mai|ja|明日の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashita no Chigo Mai|1=Ashita no Chigo Mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512132}} (at Intangible Folk Cultural Property (officially titled "Echizen no Chigo Mai") ** April 24: {{ill|Shunki Dai-sai – Hakusan Shrine Kagura|ja|春季大祭・白山神社舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunki Dai-sai – Toyohashi City, Aichi Prefecture) * 12 May ** Second Saturday in December: {{ill|Tokyo Hana Matsuri|ja|東京花祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Hana Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551169}} (in Higashikurume City, Tokyo) ==== Lunisolar (Lunisolar) ==== * On the 17th day of the 6th month: {{ill|Miyajima Kanzen-sai|ja|宮島管絃祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajima Kanzen-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551170}} (at {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} on Miyajima in Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) == References for Festivals Featuring Chigo == (See separate bibliography on Japanese festival literature) == Footnotes == {{reflist|25em|qq=}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Sexual Abuse|Sexual Abuse]] * [[:en:Child Sexual Abuse|Child Sexual Abuse]] * {{ill|Chigo Monogatari|ja|稚児物語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Monogatari|1=Chigo Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596296}} – A genre of narratives written in Japan from the medieval period to the early modern period, depicting passionate attachment between monks and chigo. {{Event-stub|qq=}} {{Japan-culture-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|稚児|version=102226228|comment=Imported full ja history}}
